Way in day logistics. So there are three different solutions that we have set up that have worked best for all of our gym lords and I'm going to share them with you. Alright, so the different set up here is just how long the meeting is, when they're doing it, how many people per time slot and who is actually running it and how much you should pay them. So solution number one is doing a 30 minute, especially if this is your first go round, this is what I recommend for most people watching this video for the first time, do a 30 minute time slot scheduling. We like Saturday early before sales because these people are already paid, so like 8am, you know 7am, 7 is kind of early, but 7.30, 8.30, a lot of people get up early anyway so they don't mind it and it kind of doesn't take up their Saturday. So you can get these people out of the way early before your sales appointment time, so if your sales appointment time started like let's say 9.30 or 10, you can still knock out all of your wands and so it doesn't cut into your sales time. So little strategy there. Three, how many per time slot? Per 30 minutes, just grab two people. That seems to work well if you're not confident about it, just start with one, but two seems to be just about the same sales, same closing percentages and it'll double your efficiency in your dollars per hour. Who would be you plus an assistant? You will want someone else there, believe me because as people come in, if you're closing a sale you're not going to want to deal with that, you're going to want that person to start with an assistant or a trainer who's going to be there to take their measurements, walk them to their meal plan, show them how to download whatever apps you're using, checking on Facebook, all that kind of stuff, you can have that person take care of before they get to you where you're going to do the nutrition consultation and the sale. So there are two distinctively different parts of the orientation. One is all the tech and administrative stuff and then the second is the nutrition component and the sale. So you would probably want you to be the star of the closer and then an assistant to play defense and get the admin work done ahead of time, greet people, get them excited, all that kind of jazz. How much do you pay this plus one? If they are not selling, you just pay them hourly and that's the way to do it. If you already have an operations manager or someone who's on salary, that would be the best person to use for this situation so that you don't have to add anything to it, to your payroll. Here's solution number two. So solution number two is seeing duration 30 minutes, scheduling again Saturday early before sales, how many per time slot two again. This time is if you already have a sales manager, this is the way to set it up. So it would be the sales manager. Mind you, if this is your first time, do not outsource this. You should get a feel for it before you tell someone else how to do it because they will not know how to do it and they won't believe you until you've done it. So let me make you money. So please do it first. After you've done it and you're kind of tired of doing it, then introduce the sales manager doing it. How do you pay them? I recommend 7.5% of supplement sales with no hourly. And so the way that you explain this is, and I want to be really clear, my guys fought to do the way in days. They loved way in days because they were basically making PT pay per hour because I had to do this, how much they make on supplement commissions. So if you're getting 7.5% and you meet with four people every hour, right, because you had two every 30 minutes, you would imagine that a bad salesman would sell three, an average $200 per person in average ticket, which would mean you would be $600 total for the hour. So three out of four people buy at four-banger. And that's an average. It'll probably be higher than that, to be honest with you. But if we're just drawing basic math for these guys, and they're getting 7.5% of that, it means they're getting $45 per hour. It's hard to beat that. So they're going to be getting paid per hour when they usually get paid per afternoon. So it's like if they work four hours, it's like them working four more days of full shifts. So they will fight for that time if they have any kind of sales inclination. And they will love the supplements because then they will also get their sponsorship because they will hit their $1,000 sales amount super easily. So just my recommendation to you, you can also not have the 7.5%. You can do 5%. You can do zero and just tell them they're going to hit it so that they get the $200 worth of supplements. It's up to you, but this has worked very, very well. And I didn't have the sponsorship piece, so I paid 7.5% to my guys, but you have an additional $200 for $8,000 that they basically get credited back. So you can use that to incentivize Harvard U-desire. Solution three, this is not recommended, but it is worth mentioning so that for completeness, this would be a 60-minute weigh-in. Again, same times Monday evening and Saturday morning, how many per time slot? 10 tends to be the max that I see done effectively. If you have more than that, people feel like they're not getting the personal attention that's necessary. So if you do sign up 25 challengers in your first week, I still would recommend doing one-on-twos and just doing it for six hours and selling every single one of them and giving them great attention. But if you are super experienced and you, there's just not a lot of scenarios where you're going to make more money in a group sale than you will in a one-on-one. But I'm just going to say this for completeness. So you'd want to cap it at 10. And this would be you slash your sales manager and assistant. So you'll still need an additional person there with you, especially when you have a larger group. The downside to this is that sometimes it's hard to get someone to show up for one single hour time slot to drive in for that. So maybe you have a trainer state after their morning sessions, but then it cuts into sales. So that's where this gets a little bit hairier. You can have the trainer come early before they do the workouts, but depends on what time you start your workouts on Saturday. These are all just different scenarios you can use, but either way, this is a long duration multi-person group orientation. The way that you would pay a sales manager is you do the same pay structure. So they're going to get 7.5% of all the sales that happen in that time slot. So if they have 10 people and they close eight, they might have an average ticket of 100. So they might close only $800 and then get 7.5%, which is probably like 60-ish bucks. So realistically, you will probably sacrifice a significant amount of, you probably sacrifice if you were to do that, probably another $1,500 in sales. But again, that is your prerogative for what you want to do with your time. Again the assistant there would be paid hourly. And so try and tack it on to the beginning or the end of something where someone's already there. So that is solution three, not my recommended way of doing it. I think it sacrifices customer experience and definitely sacrifices sales. So that would definitely be the third solution. Use number one or number two. Number one first if it's your first time. So that is the logistics of how I recommend setting up your way in day and then we will get into the actual day itself of what we're going to be covering.
